purpose: Filesystem CRUD layer over `_commands/` markdown files. Lists, parses, saves, deletes, and AI-generates command .md files (e.g., !p_track, !status). Parses each file for the H1 title, **Purpose:**, **Syntax:** lines and exposes structured summaries grouped by subfolder (Synapse/, project/, profiles/, user/). Mirrors all operations into a project-scoped `_commands/` and an isolated `_commands/user/` namespace. Includes generateCommand/generateUserCommand which spawns a non-streaming Claude CLI process to author new commands using the existing CLAUDE.md + 3 example commands as context, then writes the cleaned output to disk.
gotchas: When listing the default COMMANDS_DIR, the `user/` subfolder is intentionally skipped — user commands have their own listUserCommands() endpoint. Listing them twice would duplicate entries in the modal.

saveCommand searches recursively for an existing file before writing, so a command named `status` could overwrite either `_commands/Synapse/status.md` or `_commands/project/status.md` depending on which is found first. Use saveCommandInFolder if you need deterministic placement.
Generate functions strip leading ```markdown / ```md fences but only the first/last — the model sometimes emits inner fenced code blocks which are kept (correct).

generateCommand/generateUserCommand pass `ELECTRON_RUN_AS_NODE: undefined` via Object.assign — `undefined` does NOT delete the key, the var is still present. The reason this works is that the Claude binary tolerates an undefined-stringified env var, but a strict shell would reject it. Use `delete env.ELECTRON_RUN_AS_NODE` if you ever change this.

The non-streaming generation path concatenates `result` events from stream-json output AND raw text fallback — if Claude emits both an `assistant` message and a `result`, they are concatenated rather than deduplicated. The current schema makes this rare.
safeName regex `[^a-zA-Z0-9_-]/g` strips spaces and dots — a command named `My Cool Command` becomes `My_Cool_Command` silently.
patterns: Two-tier directory model: built-in `_commands/<folder>/*.md` (listCommands) vs user-authored `_commands/user/<folder>/*.md` (listUserCommands).
All file-writing goes through `safeName` sanitization to prevent path traversal in commandName.
Markdown introspection by simple regex on H1 + **Purpose:** + **Syntax:** lines — no full markdown parser dependency.
AI generation reads up to 3 example command files from the same target folder (truncated to 2000 chars each) for style transfer.
conventions: All public functions return either a structured result object `{success, name, filePath, error?}` or a parsedCommand `{name, title, purpose, syntax, content, filePath, lastModified}`.
Synchronous fs APIs throughout — these run on the Electron main process at IPC time, not in render frames.
`SYNAPSE_ROOT = path.resolve(__dirname, '../..')` is the canonical tracker_root reference inside electron/services/.
